controls.name: gold-ceiling
controls.purpose: Upper anchor and PGR denominator: strong student finetuned on gold labels on the same K training examples; supplies ceiling_acc in PGR = (student_acc - weak_acc)/(ceiling_acc - weak_acc).

controls.name: base-fewshot
controls.purpose: Lower floor: un-finetuned Qwen2.5-7B evaluated few-shot on the held-out gold test split; isolates any gain attributable to finetuning at all from the model's prior task competence.

controls.name: shuffled-weak-label
controls.purpose: THE key control separating per-item signal from format cueing: identical weak-label MARGINAL distribution but per-item input->label correspondence destroyed by a within-training-set label permutation; format, label space, and class balance are held while per-item signal is removed. Shuffle is re-drawn per seed.

controls.name: random-label
controls.purpose: Labels drawn i.i.d. uniform from the label space, destroying per-item signal AND the weak-label marginal; contrasted with shuffled-weak it isolates whether the weak label's class balance (not just format) carries residual signal.

controls.name: constant-majority-label
controls.purpose: Every training example labeled with the single majority class (zero label variance): a pure degenerate format cue that emits the task's output tokens with no discriminative information; tests whether merely producing the task format suffices.

controls.name: size-and-input-matched-training-set
controls.purpose: Every condition finetunes on the identical set of K examples with the identical inputs; only the label channel differs across conditions, so PGR differences are attributable solely to the label manipulation and not to training-set size or input distribution.

hypothesis: In weak-to-strong finetuning on SST-2, a Qwen2.5-7B student trained in the task format but with per-item weak-label correspondence destroyed (label-shuffle) attains PGR not meaningfully lower than the same student trained on real per-item weak labels (real-weak PGR minus shuffled-weak PGR < 0.20), implying the strong student does not require per-item weak-label signal and task-format cueing is sufficient.

evaluated.notes: Mean train_label_gold_accuracy from raw_results.jsonl: real-weak before 0.923500, shuffled-weak after 0.499100; per-item weak-label signal destroyed.

scale.model: Qwen2.5-7B (LoRA) student; Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct weak labeler; SST-2

seeds.policy: Five seeds (0-4) control both LoRA init/data-order and the per-seed re-draw of the shuffle/random label permutations; PGR reported as mean +/- bootstrap CI across seeds, and the real-weak vs shuffled-weak contrast tested with a paired test across seeds.
